name: Almond Flavored Tequila Liqueur
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons Spicy piloncillo syrup (see other recipe)
recipeInstructions:
1. Place nuts on a baking sheet in a preheated 325-degree oven and toast until dark brown, turning occasionally.
2. When done, remove from the pan to prevent overcooking.
3. Coarsely chop the nuts.
4. Place chopped nuts, vanilla bean, and cinnamon stick in a jar with tequila.
5. Cover with tequila and steep for 2 weeks in a cool, dark place, shaking gently occasionally (a murky sediment is natural).
6. Strain several times through paper coffee filters.
7. Add syrup and almond extract in small increments, tasting after each addition until the desired flavor is achieved.
8. Pour into sterilized dark-colored jars; allow to set for 2 weeks, adding more syrup if needed.
9. Serve either poured over hot coffee or over ice in brandy snifters with a twist of lemon.
(Note: Nutritional analysis per ounce: 106 calories, 4 grams fat, 3 grams carbohydrates, 0 cholesterol, 1 milligram sodium, 35 percent of calories from fat.)
